pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i walk through the house and it feels like i'm a ghost. there’s no point to any of it, just waking up and going through the motions in a world that doesn't even have him in it anymore. it feels like the light just went out forever.

pool.baseline: i cant stop thinking about how none of this actually matters. like, we're just biological accidents on a rock that's gonna be swallowed by the sun anyway. everything we do, every struggle, every feeling... it's just noise before the heat death of the universe. how can anyone actually find a reason to keep going when the math just proves it's all pointless?

pool.baseline: i don't even know why im typing this. i just feel so incredibly tired. like, not just sleep tired, but every single part of my soul is just... done. the lab results came back and everything is basically ruined. the funding is gone. i spent 5 years on this and it's just zero. everything i am is tied to this work and now there's nothing left. i just want everything to stop. i just want the noise and the pressure to finally be quiet.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the guttering flame of my own weary soul. How cruel it is, that after all the vows whispered under the willow trees, I am left only with this hollow silence. The world has grown too heavy, too cold, and the shadows seem to call to me with more kindness than any living thing ever could. I feel as though I am already a ghost, haunting the ruins of a life that no longer belongs to me. Is there not a certain grace in simply letting the darkness take what remains?

pool.baseline: i was just sitting here thinking about how nobody here actually knows who i am. like if i just stopped showing up to things or stopped responding to texts... i dont think it would even take more than a week for anyone to notice. i feel like i'm just a ghost in this city already.

pool.baseline: I just lost the last of the seed funding. The company is gone. Everything I worked for the last five years is just... evaporated. I've got nothing left to show for it except massive debt and a mountain of shame. I don't even know how I'm supposed to walk back into my parents' house or look my wife in the eye. It feels like there's no way out of this.
